revision.comment: Referenced [[ja:左義長]]2023年1月7日 (土) 22:41‎‎
revision.text:
[[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] '''The Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Lang-ja|左義長}}), also called the '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Lang-ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or January 15<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref>. It is a local event in each village or town to burn away the ''[[kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[New Year's]] decoratons as well as to pray for the New Year's best fortune. Some Sagichō festivals are related to the [[Shintoist]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan at least since the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 on the [[lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[Lantern Festival]] in [[China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[tourist attractions]] are: the [[Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated under in winter's snow in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref>; the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[seacoast]] in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]]<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref>; etc.
The Japanese-style fire festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]), [[Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == *[https://japanfreak.tokyo/dondo-yaki/ TOKYO Dondo Yaki – Burning New Year’s decoration]
[[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:New Year in Japan]] [[Category:Traditions involving fire]]

Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[Lantern Festival]] in [[China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[tourist attractions]] are: the [[Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[seacoast]] in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[Festival of fire]]

[[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[Category:New Year in Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[Category:Traditions involving fire]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]] [[Category:New]]

[[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''[[kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[New Year|New Year's]] decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[tourist attractions]] are: the [[Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[seacoast]] in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[Festival of fire]]

Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.

Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>

This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[Hilo, Hawaii]].

[[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''[[kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[New Year|New Year's]] decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[tourist attractions]] are: the [[Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[seacoast]] in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[Festival of fire]]

{{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Annual events in Japan]] [[Category:New Year in Japan]] [[Category:Traditions involving fire]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Sagichō Fire Festival]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''[[kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[New Year|New Year's]] decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[tourist attractions]] are: the [[Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[seacoast]] in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[Festival of fire]]
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''[[kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[New Year|New Year's]] decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[tourist attractions]] are: the [[Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[seacoast]] in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[Festival of fire]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == *[https://japanfreak.tokyo/dondo-yaki/ TOKYO Dondo Yaki – Burning New Year’s decoration]

[[sn:Sagichō Fire Festival]] [[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]]

revision.text:
[[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''[[kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[New Year|New Year's]] decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[:en:China|China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[tourist attractions]] are: the [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[seacoast]] in [[Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[:en:Festival of fire|Festival of fire]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == *[https://japanfreak.tokyo/dondo-yaki/ TOKYO Dondo Yaki – Burning New Year’s decoration]

}}

Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[:en:tourist attractions|tourist attractions]] are: the [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[:en:seacoast|seacoast]] in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[:en:Hilo, Hawaii|Hilo, Hawaii]].

This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[:en:Hilo, Hawaii|Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[:en:Lantern Festival|Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[:en:Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[:en:Festival of fire|Festival of fire]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q9332740]] Wikidata name: Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''[[:en:Kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[:en:New Year|New Year's]] decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[:en:Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[:en:China|China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[:en:tourist attractions|tourist attractions]] are: the [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[:en:seacoast|seacoast]] in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[:en:Hilo, Hawaii|Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[:en:Lantern Festival|Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[:en:Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[:en:Festival of fire|Festival of fire]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q9332740]] Wikidata name: Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''[[:en:Kadomatsu|gate pine]]'' and other [[:en:New Year|New Year's]] decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk [[:en:Yoshida Kenkō|Kenkō]]'s ''[[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]]''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the [[:en:Lantern_Festival|Lantern Festival]] in [[:en:China|China]].<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[:en:tourist attractions|tourist attractions]] are: the [[:en:Dōsojin|Dōsojin]] Fire Festival celebrated in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[:en:seacoast|seacoast]] in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in [[:en:Hilo, Hawaii|Hilo, Hawaii]].
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *[[:en:Lantern Festival|Lantern Festival]] (China) *[[:en:Saint John's Eve|St. John's Fire Festival]] *[[:en:Festival of fire|Festival of fire]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == *[https://japanfreak.tokyo/dondo-yaki/ TOKYO Dondo Yaki – Burning New Year’s decoration]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q9332740]] Wikidata name: Sagichō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''{{ill|Kadomatsu|en|Kadomatsu|WD=Q2667233|lt=gate pine}}'' and other {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year's}} decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk {{ill|Yoshida Kenkō|en|Yoshida Kenkō|WD=Q372772|lt=Kenkō}}'s ''{{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa}}''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the {{ill|Lantern_Festival|en|Lantern_Festival|WD=Q718636|lt=Lantern Festival}} in {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}.<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[:en:tourist attractions|tourist attractions]] are: the {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin}} Fire Festival celebrated in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[:en:seacoast|seacoast]] in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in {{ill|Hilo, Hawaii|en|Hilo, Hawaii|WD=Q216258|lt=Hilo, Hawaii}}.
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *{{ill|Lantern Festival|en|Lantern Festival|WD=Q718636|lt=Lantern Festival}} (China) *{{ill|Saint John's Eve|en|Saint John's Eve|WD=Q10398273|lt=St. John's Fire Festival}} *{{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Festival of fire}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == *[https://japanfreak.tokyo/dondo-yaki/ TOKYO Dondo Yaki – Burning New Year’s decoration]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sagichō]][[en:Sagichō Fire Festival]][[ja:左義長]][[pl:Sagichō]]
[[File:大磯左義長 - panoramio (8).jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Sagichō" Fire Festival in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]]]] [[File:Hatsuakarikago lit up by flames from burning Shadan in Nozawa Onsen.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The "Dōsojin" Fire Festival in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]]]] The '''Sagichō Fire Festival''' ({{Langx|ja|左義長}}), also called '''Dondoyaki''' ({{Langx|ja|どんど焼き}}) or by other names, is a festival celebrated in Japan, usually on January 14 or 15.<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/uk/inspiration/10-famous-japanese-fire-festivals/ 10 Famous Japanese Fire Festivals (2023)]</ref> During this local event, town or village residents burn their ''{{ill|Kadomatsu|en|Kadomatsu|WD=Q2667233|lt=gate pine|1=Kadomatsu}}'' and other {{ill|New Year|en|New Year|WD=Q34812|lt=New Year's|1=New Year}} decorations, as well as to pray for good fortune in the new year. Some Sagichō festivals are held at [[Shinto]] shrines.
The Sagichō fire festival has been celebrated in Japan since at least the 13th century, as it is mentioned in monk {{ill|Yoshida Kenkō|en|Yoshida Kenkō|WD=Q372772|lt=Kenkō|1=Yoshida Kenkō}}'s ''{{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa|1=Tsurezuregusa}}''. Because it used to be celebrated on January 14 or 15 in the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]], its origin may have been related to the {{ill|Lantern_Festival|en|Lantern_Festival|WD=Q718636|lt=Lantern Festival|1=Lantern Festival}} in {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}.<ref>[https://www.urarekishi.com/2022/12/Sagicho.html Getting to know the origin of Sagicho Festival] (in Japanese)</ref>
Some Sagichō fire festivals that have become famous as [[:en:tourist attractions|tourist attractions]] are: the {{ill|Dōsojin|en|Dōsojin|WD=Q269515|lt=Dōsojin|1=Dōsojin}} Fire Festival celebrated in [[:en:Nozawaonsen, Nagano|Nozawaonsen, Nagano]];<ref>[https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/2218/ Nozawa Fire Festival]</ref> the Sagicho Fire Festival held on the [[:en:seacoast|seacoast]] in [[:en:Oiso, Kanagawa|Oiso, Kanagawa]];<ref>[https://en.japantravel.com/kanagawa/oiso-s-sagicho-festival/3763 Oiso's Sagicho Festival (Oisho, Kanagawa)]</ref> etc.
This festival is also celebrated at Japanese Shintoist shrines overseas, such as the Hilo Daijingu ([[:ja:|ヒロ大神宮]]) in {{ill|Hilo, Hawaii|en|Hilo, Hawaii|WD=Q216258|lt=Hilo, Hawaii|1=Hilo, Hawaii}}.
== See also == *[[Burning of the Character Big|Festival of Burning the Character Big]] (Japan) *{{ill|Lantern Festival|en|Lantern Festival|WD=Q718636|lt=Lantern Festival|1=Lantern Festival}} (China) *{{ill|Saint John's Eve|en|Saint John's Eve|WD=Q10398273|lt=St. John's Fire Festival|1=Saint John's Eve}} *{{ill|Festival of fire|en|Festival of fire|WD=Q3135724|lt=Festival of fire|1=Fire Festival}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links == *[https://japanfreak.tokyo/dondo-yaki/ TOKYO Dondo Yaki – Burning New Year’s decoration]
